title: [refactor] Update systemd service and documentation
description:
Update .sandcastle/systemd/sandcastle.service:
- Add TimeoutStopSec=630 (10min + 30s buffer)
- Remove stale MAX_ITERATIONS comments

Update .sandcastle/systemd/README.md:
- Document infinite poll loop
- Document SIGTERM / graceful shutdown behavior
- Document heartbeat logs and journalctl reading
- Document pino JSON log format

title: [refactor] Install vitest and add unit tests
description:
Install vitest dev dependency.

Create .sandcastle/helpers/concurrency.test.mts:
- Concurrency limit is respected
- All promises settled even when some reject
- Results in correct order

Create .sandcastle/helpers/issues.test.mts:
- getOpenIssues success path (mock zx output)
- getOpenIssues error path returns empty array

Update package.json test script to vitest run.
Add vitest.config.ts with NodeNext resolution.

title: [refactor] Refactor main.mts entry point for daemon behavior
description:
Wrap orchestration in async main() function.

- Replace MAX_ITERATIONS for loop with while (true) and manual iteration counter
- Add SIGTERM handler: set shouldShutdown flag, 10-minute fallback timer
- Heartbeat log at top of each loop iteration
- After Phase 3, check shouldShutdown; if true, break loop and exit
- main().catch(err => { logger.fatal(err); process.exit(1) })
- Remove all now-extracted inline code
- Target: main.mts under 80 lines

title: [refactor] Extract merge phase to phases/merge.mts
description:
Create .sandcastle/phases/merge.mts exporting runMergePhase().

Takes completed issues and branches, runs merger sandbox, returns void. Accepts logger and sandbox provider.

Use branchStrategy: merge-to-head for the merger run.

Update .sandcastle/main.mts to call runMergePhase().

title: [refactor] Extract execute phase to phases/execute.mts
description:
Create .sandcastle/phases/execute.mts exporting runExecutionPhase().

Takes issue list, creates sandboxes per issue, runs implementer then reviewer, collects completed branches. Uses runWithConcurrencyLimit internally. Accepts logger, sandbox provider, hooks, copyToWorktree.

Update .sandcastle/main.mts to call runExecutionPhase().

title: [refactor] Extract plan phase to phases/plan.mts
description:
Create .sandcastle/phases/plan.mts exporting runPlanner().

Performs sandbox run, extracts <plan> XML block, parses JSON, validates with PlannerOutputSchema, returns issue list. Accepts logger and sandbox provider as args.

Update .sandcastle/main.mts to call runPlanner().

title: [refactor] Extract types and schemas to types.mts
description:
Create .sandcastle/types.mts with:
- BeadsIssueSchema
- PlannerOutputSchema
- Exported BeadsIssue type

Update .sandcastle/main.mts to import schemas/types from types.mts.

title: [refactor] Extract concurrency helper with simplified implementation
description:
Create .sandcastle/helpers/concurrency.mts with runWithConcurrencyLimit().

Rewrite internals to use Set<Promise<void>> and .finally() instead of nested Promise.race cleanup loop.

Update .sandcastle/main.mts to import from helpers/concurrency.mts.

title: [refactor] Extract issue helpers to helpers/issues.mts
description:
Create .sandcastle/helpers/issues.mts with:
- getOpenIssues()
- waitUntilThereAreOpenIssues()

Replace console.log calls with logger.info/logger.error from config.
Update .sandcastle/main.mts to import from helpers/issues.mts.

title: [refactor] Install pino + pino-pretty, extract config module
description:
Install pino and pino-pretty. Create .sandcastle/config.mts with:
- resolveHostPath()
- sandboxMounts construction and validation
- readOpencodeApiKey()
- sandboxProvider initialization
- Pino logger (pretty when TTY, JSON otherwise)
- Constants: MAX_PARALLEL_TASKS, POLL_INTERVAL_MS, GRACEFUL_SHUTDOWN_MS

Update .sandcastle/main.mts to import from config.mts instead of defining inline.

title: Make merge phase sequential — merge one branch at a time
description:
## Problem Statement

Currently, the merger phase passes all completed branches to a single `sandcastle.run()` call. One agent receives the full list of branches and merges them all at once. If a merge fails or introduces conflicts, the agent must reason about multiple branches simultaneously, which increases complexity and makes failure isolation harder.

## Proposed Solution

Process branches sequentially instead of in a batch. After the execute phase completes, iterate over each completed branch one at a time and merge it into the target branch before proceeding to the next.

## Open Questions

- Should each branch get its own `sandcastle.run()` call, or should one agent run handle them sequentially?
- If a sequential merge fails (conflicts), should the process abort, skip that branch, or escalate to human review?
- Should there be a review step between each merge?
- How does this interact with the `merge-to-head` branch strategy for the merger sandbox?

## Out of Scope

- Changing how the execute phase works
- Changing the review logic inside the execute phase

title: Refactor .sandcastle/main.mts for readability and daemonize the orchestration loop
description:
## Problem Statement

.sandcastle/main.mts has grown into a single 260-line file that mixes configuration, helper functions, type schemas, and three distinct orchestration phases (plan, execute, merge) inside a top-level `for` loop. The file is hard to read, hard to navigate, and hard to test. There is no clear entry point (code runs on import), no separation of concerns, and the `runWithConcurrencyLimit` helper uses a non-idiomatic `Promise.race` cleanup pattern that is difficult to follow.

Additionally, the current loop is capped at `MAX_ITERATIONS = 10`, which means the service must be restarted manually to continue processing. The user wants this to run as a long-lived systemd service that polls forever.

## Solution

Refactor main.mts into a small, focused module tree:

- `.sandcastle/config.mts` — environment, constants, pino logger (with pretty-print for TTY)
- `.sandcastle/types.mts` — shared Zod schemas and TypeScript types
- `.sandcastle/helpers/concurrency.mts` — cleaned-up `runWithConcurrencyLimit`
- `.sandcastle/helpers/issues.mts` — `getOpenIssues`, `waitUntilThereAreOpenIssues`
- `.sandcastle/phases/plan.mts` — Phase 1: planner sandbox run
- `.sandcastle/phases/execute.mts` — Phase 2: per-issue implement + review
- `.sandcastle/phases/merge.mts` — Phase 3: merge completed branches
- `.sandcastle/main.mts` — thin orchestrator: `main()` function, `while (true)` loop, SIGTERM handling, heartbeat logging

Replace `MAX_ITERATIONS` with an infinite `while (true)` loop. Add a `SIGTERM` handler that allows the current iteration to finish gracefully but forces exit after 10 minutes. Log a heartbeat at the start of each iteration. Use pino for structured logging; pretty-print when stdout is a TTY, JSON otherwise (systemd journal). Update the systemd service file and README to document the new daemon behavior.

## Commits

Each commit leaves the codebase in a working state.

**Commit 1: Install pino and pino-pretty, extract config module**
- `pnpm add pino` and `pnpm add -D pino-pretty`
- Create `.sandcastle/config.mts` containing:
  - `resolveHostPath()`
  - `sandboxMounts` construction and validation
  - `readOpencodeApiKey()`
  - `sandboxProvider` initialization
  - Pino logger setup (pretty transport when `process.stdout.isTTY` is true, raw JSON otherwise)
  - Constants: `MAX_PARALLEL_TASKS`, `POLL_INTERVAL_MS`, `GRACEFUL_SHUTDOWN_MS`
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to import config instead of defining inline

**Commit 2: Extract types and schemas to types.mts**
- Create `.sandcastle/types.mts` with `BeadsIssueSchema`, `PlannerOutputSchema`, and exported `BeadsIssue` type
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to import schemas/types from `types.mts`

**Commit 3: Extract issue helpers to helpers/issues.mts**
- Create `.sandcastle/helpers/issues.mts` with `getOpenIssues()` and `waitUntilThereAreOpenIssues()`
- Replace `console.log` calls inside helpers with `logger.info` / `logger.error` from config
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to import from `helpers/issues.mts`

**Commit 4: Extract concurrency helper with simplified implementation**
- Create `.sandcastle/helpers/concurrency.mts` with `runWithConcurrencyLimit()`
- Rewrite the internals to use a `Set<Promise<void>>` and `.finally(() => executing.delete(p))` instead of the nested `Promise.race` cleanup loop
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to import from `helpers/concurrency.mts`

**Commit 5: Extract plan phase to phases/plan.mts**
- Create `.sandcastle/phases/plan.mts` exporting `runPlanner()`
- The function performs the sandbox run, extracts the `<plan>` XML block, parses JSON, validates with `PlannerOutputSchema`, and returns the issue list
- Accepts logger and sandbox provider as arguments
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to call `runPlanner()` instead of inline code

**Commit 6: Extract execute phase to phases/execute.mts**
- Create `.sandcastle/phases/execute.mts` exporting `runExecutionPhase()`
- The function takes the issue list, creates sandboxes per issue, runs implementer then reviewer, collects completed branches, and returns the results
- Uses `runWithConcurrencyLimit` internally
- Accepts logger, sandbox provider, hooks, and `copyToWorktree` as arguments
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to call `runExecutionPhase()` instead of inline code

**Commit 7: Extract merge phase to phases/merge.mts**
- Create `.sandcastle/phases/merge.mts` exporting `runMergePhase()`
- The function takes completed issues and branches, runs the merger sandbox, and returns void
- Accepts logger and sandbox provider as arguments
- Update `.sandcastle/main.mts` to call `runMergePhase()` instead of inline code

**Commit 8: Refactor main.mts entry point for daemon behavior**
- Wrap the orchestration loop in an async `main()` function
- Replace `for (let iteration = 1; iteration <= MAX_ITERATIONS; iteration++)` with `while (true)` and a manual `iteration` counter
- Add `SIGTERM` handler:
  - Set a `shouldShutdown` flag to true
  - Start a 10-minute timer; if the loop hasn't exited by then, call `process.exit(1)`
- At the top of each loop iteration, log a heartbeat via pino (include iteration count and process uptime)
- After Phase 3, check `shouldShutdown`; if true, log shutdown message and break the loop
- Add `main().catch(err => { logger.fatal(err); process.exit(1); })` at module level
- Remove all now-extracted inline code from main.mts

**Commit 9: Install vitest and add unit tests**
- `pnpm add -D vitest`
- Create `.sandcastle/helpers/concurrency.test.mts`:
  - Test that `runWithConcurrencyLimit` respects the concurrency limit
  - Test that all promises are settled even when some reject
  - Test that results are in the correct order
- Create `.sandcastle/helpers/issues.test.mts`:
  - Test `getOpenIssues` success path (mock `zx` output)
  - Test `getOpenIssues` error path returns empty array
- Update `package.json` test script to `vitest run`
- Add `vitest.config.ts` with `NodeNext` module resolution if needed

**Commit 10: Update systemd service and documentation**
- Update `.sandcastle/systemd/sandcastle.service`:
  - Add `TimeoutStopSec=630` (10 minutes + 30 second buffer for graceful shutdown)
  - Remove stale comments about `MAX_ITERATIONS`
- Update `.sandcastle/systemd/README.md`:
  - Document the infinite poll loop
  - Document SIGTERM / graceful shutdown behavior
  - Document heartbeat logs and how to read them with `journalctl`
  - Document pino JSON log format

## Decision Document

- **Module boundaries:** Config, types, helpers, and phases each get their own file. No cross-import cycles: main imports everything; phases import helpers and types; helpers import types and config.
- **Logger interface:** All modules accept a pino `Logger` instance as an argument rather than importing it directly. This keeps modules testable (pass a silent test logger) and avoids hidden global state.
- **Entry point shape:** `main.mts` exports no named exports; it calls `main()` at module load and handles the promise. This is acceptable for a CLI entry point. If tests ever need to import the loop logic, `main()` can be exported later.
- **SIGTERM behavior:** The process handles SIGTERM by setting a boolean flag. The current iteration completes (plan → execute → merge), then the loop checks the flag and exits cleanly. A 10-minute fallback timer ensures the process dies even if an agent hangs.
- **systemd integration:** `Restart=on-failure` is kept. `TimeoutStopSec=630` gives systemd awareness of the graceful shutdown window. The process itself also enforces the 10-minute limit to avoid relying solely on systemd.
- **Pretty print:** Detected via `process.stdout.isTTY`. When running under systemd, stdout is not a TTY, so logs are raw JSON suitable for `journalctl -o json`.
- **Infinite loop:** `while (true)` replaces `MAX_ITERATIONS`. The only exit conditions are SIGTERM, an unhandled exception (which crashes the process and triggers systemd restart), or a catastrophic error inside the loop that isn't caught.
- **Error handling in the loop:** Errors thrown during plan/execute/merge are logged at `fatal` level and rethrown, causing the process to crash. systemd restarts the service. This matches the user's preference for "crash and let systemd restart it."

## Testing Decisions

- **Test external behavior, not implementation details:** Tests for `runWithConcurrencyLimit` verify that the limit is respected and that the returned array preserves input order. They do not inspect the internal `Set` or promise mechanics.
- **Mock external dependencies:** `getOpenIssues` tests mock the `zx` shell invocation and `bd` JSON output. No tests hit the real `bd` database or network.
- **Test modules:**
  - `.sandcastle/helpers/concurrency.mts`
  - `.sandcastle/helpers/issues.mts`
- **Prior art:** There are currently no tests in the codebase. This commit establishes the testing convention using vitest.
- **Out of scope for tests:** Phases (plan, execute, merge) are not unit-tested in this refactor because they depend on the `sandcastle.run()` SDK and Docker sandboxes, which are impractical to mock meaningfully without significant extra work. They can be covered by integration tests in future work.

## Out of Scope

- Extracting the prompt files (`.sandcastle/*-prompt.md`) into a different structure
- Changing the sandcastle agent model (`opencode-go/kimi-k2.6`)
- Changing the review logic (e.g., running reviewer only when commits exist)
- Adding integration or end-to-end tests for the full orchestration loop
- Docker image changes or sandbox provider changes
- Changing the `copyToWorktree` or `hooks` definitions
- Replacing `zx` with a different shell library
- Metrics, alerting, or health-check endpoints beyond the heartbeat log line

## Further Notes

- The existing `.sandcastle/logs/` directory and its contents are untouched.
- The `Containerfile`, `.env.example`, `.gitignore`, and prompt files remain unchanged.
- After this refactor, `.sandcastle/main.mts` should be under 80 lines of orchestration code.
